scope: bounded single-writer runtime-event ledger with batched commits, stable ledger identity, conflict preservation, retention, and shutdown barriers
durable SSE cursor replay, replay-gap controls, pending-delivery semantics, reconnect status hydration, and TUI Last-Event-ID retention
coalesced atomic session saves with unique temporary files and stale-writer serialization
read-only busy-heartbeat coalescing and independent read-only tool scheduling characterization

verification.command: uv run pytest -q tests/api/test_runtime_event_ledger.py tests/api/test_runtime_event_ledger_writer.py tests/api/test_opencode_events_service.py tests/api/test_sse_and_status_scoping.py tests/system/test_session_save_sync.py tests/api/test_concurrent_session_isolation.py tests/system/test_context_lifecycle_records.py tests/system/test_context_borrowing_integration.py tests/system/test_context_autoload.py tests/web/test_runtime_storage.py
bun run typecheck && bun test test/tui/penguin-event-stream.test.ts test/tui/penguin-send.test.ts

notes: No live provider or production port 9000 was used.
Live event admission is bounded and nonblocking; only the writer thread performs SQLite append, retention, WAL checkpoint, and cleanup work.
Pending live SSE frames never advance the TUI durable cursor; only replayed committed rows do.
Identical same-id events remain idempotent; same-id/different-payload events are preserved under deterministic conflict ids.
CWM v2 history selection, slimming, summarization, retrieval, budgeting, and compaction remain deferred.
